name: plugin-structure
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a plugin", "scaffold a plugin", "understand plugin structure", "organize plugin components", "set up plugin.json", "use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}", "add commands/agents/skills/hooks", "configure auto-discovery", or needs guidance on plugin directory layout, manifest configuration, component organization, file naming conventions, or Claude Code plugin architecture best practices.
capabilities: Explains the standard plugin directory structure with required and optional directories
Documents plugin.json manifest fields with examples for minimal and complete configurations
Describes component organization patterns for commands, agents, skills, and hooks
Details the auto-discovery mechanism for automatic component loading
Provides guidance on using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portable path references
Includes complete example plugins from minimal to advanced patterns
author: anthropics
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill from the official Anthropic repository (anthropics/claude-plugins-official). All 583 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the detected patterns are markdown code block delimiters, example configurations, and educational content in documentation files, not executable code with security implications.
valueStatement: Developing Claude Code plugins requires understanding the correct directory structure, manifest configuration, and component organization patterns. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on plugin architecture so you can build maintainable plugins that follow Claude Code conventions.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files on your system
Does not provide interactive plugin scaffolding or code generation
Does not install or manage plugins in your Claude Code environment
Focuses on architecture guidance, not specific implementation details for individual plugins

useCases.title: Build Your First Plugin
useCases.description: New developers learning Claude Code plugin structure need clear guidance on directory layout, manifest setup, and component organization.

useCases.title: Establish Team Standards
useCases.description: Teams adopting Claude Code need consistent plugin architecture patterns for maintainable, scalable plugin development across projects.

useCases.title: Publish to Marketplace
useCases.description: Authors preparing plugins for the marketplace need to understand conventions and best practices for community adoption.

promptTemplates.title: Create Plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I structure a new Claude Code plugin? What directories do I need and where should the plugin.json file go?

promptTemplates.title: Configure Manifest
promptTemplates.prompt: What fields are required in plugin.json? Show me how to configure custom paths for commands, agents, and hooks.

promptTemplates.title: Organize Components
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I organize commands, agents, skills, and hooks in my plugin? What naming conventions should I follow?

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me an advanced plugin example with MCP servers, hooks, and shared scripts. How do I use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} correctly?

bestPractices: Use kebab-case for all directory names, file names, and component names for consistency across platforms.
Keep the plugin.json manifest lean and rely on auto-discovery for standard component directories.
Always use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} instead of hardcoded absolute paths to make your plugin portable.
antiPatterns: Hardcoding absolute paths like /Users/name/plugins/ - this breaks portability across different systems.
Nesting component directories inside .claude-plugin/ - components must be at the plugin root level.
Skipping the plugin.json manifest or placing it in the wrong location - Claude Code requires it in .claude-plugin/

faq.question: What is the minimum required for a Claude Code plugin?
faq.answer: A minimal plugin needs only a .claude-plugin/plugin.json with a name field, and at least one component like a command in the commands/ directory.

faq.question: Where should I place the plugin.json file?
faq.answer: The plugin.json must be in a .claude-plugin directory at your plugin root. Components like commands, agents, and skills go at the root level, not inside .claude-plugin.

faq.question: How do I use portable paths in my plugin?
faq.answer: Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} as a placeholder for your plugin installation directory. This environment variable is set automatically and works across all platforms.

faq.question: What components can a plugin contain?
faq.answer: Plugins can include commands (slash commands), agents (subagent definitions), skills (auto-activating capabilities), hooks (event handlers), MCP servers, and helper scripts.

faq.question: How does auto-discovery work?
faq.answer: Claude Code automatically finds components by scanning standard directories: commands/, agents/, skills/, and loading hooks.json. No manual registration required for standard layouts.

faq.question: Can I use custom paths for components?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify custom paths in plugin.json. These supplement rather than replace default directories. Custom paths must start with ./ and be relative to the plugin root.
